articles.headline: Eleven Dead in Delhi Building Collapse: Calls for Stricter Urban Planning and Construction Norms
articles.explainer:
• A residential building in a densely populated area of New Delhi collapsed, killing eleven people and injuring several others.
• The incident has reignited concerns over unauthorized construction, structural integrity of old buildings, and lax enforcement of building codes in urban areas.
• Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause, with initial reports suggesting structural weakness and illegal modifications.
• Experts are calling for an immediate review of urban planning policies, disaster management protocols, and accountability mechanisms for developers and municipal bodies.
• The tragedy highlights the urgent need for comprehensive urban renewal programs and safety audits across major Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization
Problems and remedies
Disaster and Disaster Management
Social empowerment
Role of civil society
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: New Delhi.
Number of casualties: Eleven dead, several injured.
Key issues highlighted: Unauthorized construction, old infrastructure, enforcement of building codes.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in urban governance and infrastructure development in India, using the Delhi building collapse as a case study.
Discuss measures to improve construction safety, ensure structural integrity of buildings, and enhance disaster response mechanisms in Indian cities.
Examine the role of municipal corporations, urban development authorities, and regulatory bodies in preventing such tragedies and ensuring accountability.
articles.imageDescription: Rescue workers and emergency services at the site of a collapsed residential building in New Delhi, with debris scattered around.
articles.trivia: Delhi is one of the most densely populated cities globally, with a significant portion of its housing stock being old or informally constructed.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Problems of urbanization and their remedies

articles.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision comes as inflation shows signs of moderation, though global uncertainties continue to pose risks to economic stability.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band of 2-6%.
• Analysts suggest the central bank is adopting a cautious wait-and-watch approach, assessing the impact of previous rate hikes and global economic trends.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were also discussed, with the RBI affirming its stance on managing surplus liquidity effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy
Monetary policy
Inflation
Growth and development
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5% (unchanged).
Inflation target band for RBI: 2-6%.
MPC meetings are held at least four times a year.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for economic growth and inflation in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative of fostering economic growth in the current global economic climate.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation and external shocks.
articles.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo, symbolizing financial stability and economic governance.
articles.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

articles.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Cooperation in Space Sector with New Joint Missions
articles.explainer:
• India and France announced enhanced collaboration in space technology, including new joint satellite missions for climate monitoring and maritime surveillance.
• The partnership aims to leverage ISRO's launch capabilities and CNES's expertise in satellite technology and data analysis.
• Key areas of cooperation include Earth observation, deep space exploration, and human spaceflight programs.
• This strategic alliance underscores both nations' commitment to sustainable space utilization and addressing global challenges like climate change.
• Training and exchange programs for scientists and engineers are also part of the expanded agreement, fostering knowledge transfer.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Partner countries: India and France.
Key space agencies involved: ISRO (India) and CNES (France).
Focus areas: Climate monitoring, maritime surveillance, Earth observation, deep space, human spaceflight.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-France cooperation in the space sector for India's strategic autonomy and its role in addressing global challenges.
Discuss how bilateral space partnerships contribute to technological advancement, economic growth, and diplomatic influence for both nations.
Evaluate the potential for India to emerge as a global leader in space technology through such international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: A composite image showing the flags of India and France with satellites orbiting Earth in the background, symbolizing space cooperation.
articles.trivia: India and France have a long-standing history of cooperation in space, dating back to the 1960s, making France one of India's oldest partners in this domain.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Orders Comprehensive Review of Electoral Bond Scheme Implementation
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India directed the Election Commission and relevant government agencies to submit a comprehensive report on the implementation and impact of the now-scrapped Electoral Bond scheme.
• This directive follows concerns raised about transparency in political funding and the potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The court emphasized the need for full disclosure of all data related to electoral bonds, including donor and recipient details, which were previously withheld.
• The ruling aims to ensure accountability and uphold the principles of free and fair elections, crucial for democratic integrity.
• This move could pave the way for further legislative or judicial interventions to reform political funding mechanisms in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bond scheme was previously scrapped by the Supreme Court.
The court has now ordered a comprehensive review of its implementation.
Key bodies involved: Election Commission, government agencies.
mainsPoints: Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's directive on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and democratic governance in India.
Discuss the challenges in regulating political funding in India and suggest reforms to enhance accountability and curb illegal practices.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in safeguarding democratic principles and ensuring fair electoral processes.
articles.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing justice and judicial oversight in democratic processes.
articles.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme, introduced in 2017, allowed individuals and corporate entities to donate to political parties anonymously, a provision that faced significant legal scrutiny.

articles.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Pledges
articles.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is witnessing significant progress, attracting substantial investment pledges from both domestic and international players.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• New policy incentives and a streamlined regulatory framework are being developed to facilitate faster project execution and technology adoption.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its applications in sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel are underway.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its ambitious climate targets and transition towards a sustainable energy future.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Objective of the mission: Make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources.
Applications include refining, fertilizers, steel, and transportation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security, climate goals, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into various industrial sectors in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies, private investments, and technological innovation in achieving the mission's objectives.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration depicting green hydrogen production facilities with wind turbines and solar panels in the background, symbolizing renewable energy.
articles.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, a process called electrolysis.

articles.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focusing on Lunar Sample Return
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, targeting a lunar sample return.
• This mission aims to retrieve lunar soil and rock samples for detailed scientific analysis, providing insights into the Moon's origin and evolution.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a complex multi-component mission, potentially involving a lander, rover, and an ascent module to bring samples back to Earth.
• The mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1 and Chandrayaan-2, advancing India's capabilities in planetary exploration.
• International collaborations are also being explored to enhance the mission's scientific scope and technological feasibility.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4's primary objective: Lunar sample return.
Previous Chandrayaan missions: Chandrayaan-1 (2008), Chandrayaan-2 (2019).
Key components: Lander, Rover, Ascent Module (expected).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of India's lunar exploration program, particularly missions like Chandrayaan-4, for scientific advancement and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the broader implications of India's growing capabilities in space technology for its economic development and international collaborations.
articles.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Chandrayaan-4 lander and rover on the lunar surface, collecting samples, with Earth visible in the distance.
articles.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was by the Soviet Union's Luna 16 in 1970.

articles.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure Model at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
articles.explainer:
• India advocated for the global adoption of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
• The Indian DPI model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, has been lauded for its success in driving financial inclusion and digital governance.
• India emphasized the potential of DPIs to accelerate sustainable development goals in developing nations by providing accessible and interoperable digital services.
• Discussions focused on funding mechanisms, technical assistance, and policy frameworks to help other countries implement similar digital ecosystems.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of open-source, inclusive technological solutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's DPI examples: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Forum where India championed DPI: G20 Finance Ministers meeting.
DPI aims to accelerate Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
mainsPoints: Elaborate on the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and analyze how India's model has contributed to financial inclusion and digital governance.
Discuss the potential and challenges of replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries, considering socio-economic and regulatory differences.
Examine India's growing role as a leader in digital innovation and its implications for global technological cooperation and soft power.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, with the G20 logo in the background.
articles.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 130 billion transactions in 2024, making it one of the most successful digital payment systems globally.

articles.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Combat Coastal Erosion on Eastern Coast
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new comprehensive scheme to mitigate coastal erosion along India's eastern coastline.
• The scheme focuses on a multi-pronged approach, including natural solutions like mangrove restoration and artificial interventions such as seawalls and groynes.
• Climate change-induced sea-level rise and increased frequency of cyclones are exacerbating coastal erosion, threatening livelihoods and ecosystems.
• The initiative will also involve community participation and capacity building for local populations to adapt to changing coastal dynamics.
• Funding will be allocated for research and development into innovative erosion control technologies and monitoring systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Focus of the scheme: Coastal erosion mitigation on India's eastern coastline.
Approaches: Mangrove restoration (natural), seawalls, groynes (artificial).
Causes of erosion: Sea-level rise, cyclones.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and consequences of coastal erosion in India, particularly along the eastern coast, and discuss the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the importance of integrating traditional knowledge with scientific approaches in coastal zone management and disaster preparedness.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale environmental protection schemes and ensuring community participation for long-term sustainability.
articles.imageDescription: A coastline showing a combination of restored mangroves and engineered structures like seawalls protecting against erosion, with the sea in the background.
articles.trivia: Approximately one-third of India's coastline is experiencing varying degrees of erosion, with the eastern coast being particularly vulnerable due to its geological features and exposure to Bay of Bengal cyclones.

articles.headline: India Pledges Continued Humanitarian Assistance to Afghanistan Amidst Crisis
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to providing humanitarian aid to Afghanistan, focusing on food security, medical supplies, and educational support.
• The pledge comes amidst ongoing humanitarian crisis in Afghanistan, exacerbated by economic challenges and political instability.
• India emphasized its 'people-first' approach, ensuring that aid reaches the most vulnerable sections of the Afghan population directly.
• Discussions with international partners are underway to ensure effective delivery mechanisms and coordination of relief efforts.
• While maintaining a cautious stance on political recognition, India continues to engage on humanitarian grounds, reflecting its neighborhood first policy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Recipient country: Afghanistan.
India's aid focus: Food, medical supplies, education.
India's foreign policy principle: 'Neighbourhood First'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complexities of India's foreign policy towards Afghanistan in the current geopolitical scenario, balancing humanitarian concerns with strategic interests.
Discuss the challenges in delivering humanitarian aid to conflict-affected regions and the importance of international cooperation in such efforts.
Evaluate the 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its application in addressing crises in India's immediate vicinity.
articles.imageDescription: A symbolic image of hands extending aid to a silhouette of Afghanistan, representing India's humanitarian efforts.
articles.trivia: India has historically been a significant development partner for Afghanistan, undertaking various infrastructure projects and providing capacity building support.

articles.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focuses on Interoperability
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced an expansion of its services, aiming to create a seamless online platform for healthcare.
• Key focus areas include enhancing the interoperability of digital health records and integrating more private healthcare providers into the ecosystem.
• The mission seeks to empower citizens with greater control over their health data and facilitate easier access to healthcare services across the country.
• New features are being rolled out, such as advanced health locker functionalities and telemedicine integration with primary health centers.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare delivery efficiency in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Key aspects: Interoperability of health records, integration of private providers.
Empowers citizens with control over health data.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to transform India's healthcare landscape and address existing disparities in access and quality.
Discuss the challenges associated with implementing a national digital health mission, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services and promote the adoption of ABDM across diverse socio-economic groups.
articles.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected digital health services, with a mobile phone displaying a health app and a doctor consulting via telemedicine.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021, building on the pilot project initiated in six Union Territories.

articles.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty Issues
articles.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan met for technical discussions regarding various aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The talks focused on resolving disputes related to the design of hydroelectric projects on the western rivers, as per the treaty provisions.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the IWT, a landmark agreement governing transboundary river water sharing.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the engagement is seen as crucial for maintaining dialogue and managing water resources cooperatively.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, established under the treaty, facilitates these annual meetings and technical exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Indus Water Treaty (IWT) signed between India and Pakistan.
Western Rivers: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Pakistan's exclusive use).
Eastern Rivers: Ravi, Beas, Sutlej (India's exclusive use).
Forum for talks: Permanent Indus Commission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Water Treaty in India-Pakistan relations and the challenges in its implementation amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
Analyze the role of transboundary water agreements in fostering regional cooperation and preventing conflicts over shared natural resources.
Evaluate the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the long-term viability and effectiveness of the IWT.
articles.imageDescription: A map illustrating the Indus River System and the division of rivers as per the Indus Water Treaty between India and Pakistan.
articles.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, brokered by the World Bank, was signed in 1960 and is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

articles.headline: PM-KISAN Scheme Undergoes Enhanced Beneficiary Verification to Curb Irregularities
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government initiated an enhanced verification drive for beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The move aims to eliminate ineligible beneficiaries and curb irregularities, ensuring that financial aid reaches genuine farmers.
• Leveraging Aadhaar-based authentication and land record digitization, the verification process seeks to improve transparency and efficiency.
• Farmers found ineligible are being asked to return the disbursed funds, with provisions for recovery mechanisms.
• This measure is part of broader efforts to streamline direct benefit transfer (DBT) schemes and optimize public expenditure.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: PM-KISAN scheme provides income support to farmer families.
Verification methods: Aadhaar-based authentication, land record digitization.
Aim: Eliminate ineligible beneficiaries and curb irregularities.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the effectiveness of direct benefit transfer (DBT) schemes like PM-KISAN in achieving their objectives and addressing agrarian distress.
Discuss the challenges in identifying genuine beneficiaries and preventing leakages in welfare schemes, and suggest technological and administrative solutions.
Analyze the broader implications of enhanced verification drives for public expenditure management and the promotion of good governance in India.
articles.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field, with a digital overlay symbolizing direct benefit transfer and Aadhaar verification, highlighting the modernization of agricultural support.
articles.trivia: PM-KISAN scheme provides an income support of ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months to eligible farmer families.

articles.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Review of Implementation Progress and Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review highlighted advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Key challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and ensuring equitable access to quality education across all regions.
• Stakeholders, including state governments, educational institutions, and civil society organizations, participated in the consultative meeting.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to the NEP's transformative vision, emphasizing flexible learning pathways and multidisciplinary education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims for comprehensive reform in education.
Progress areas: Curriculum, vocational education, digital learning.
Challenges: Infrastructure, teacher training, equitable access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reshaping India's education system and fostering a knowledge-based society.
Discuss the major challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning resource allocation, teacher capacity building, and digital divide.
Suggest strategies for state governments and educational institutions to overcome these challenges and ensure the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
articles.imageDescription: A group of diverse students in a modern classroom setting, with digital learning tools, symbolizing the holistic and inclusive vision of NEP 2020.
articles.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, succeeding the policies of 1968 and 1986 (modified in 1992).

articles.headline: India Aims to Strengthen Domestic Chip Design Ecosystem with New Incentives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology announced new incentives to bolster India's domestic semiconductor chip design ecosystem.
• The initiative aims to attract global players and nurture local talent, reducing India's reliance on imported chips.
• Focus areas include providing financial support for R&D, establishing state-of-the-art design centers, and fostering academia-industry collaboration.
• This move is critical for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing and its ambition to become a global semiconductor hub.
• Skill development programs and specialized courses in VLSI design and embedded systems are also being promoted.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Objective: Strengthen domestic semiconductor chip design ecosystem.
Measures: Financial support, design centers, academia-industry collaboration.
Goal: Reduce reliance on imported chips, achieve strategic autonomy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor chip design and manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic growth and national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in attracting global semiconductor investments and nurturing local talent in this highly capital-intensive sector.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentive schemes and policy frameworks in achieving India's ambition to become a global semiconductor hub.
articles.imageDescription: A stylized microchip with circuits, representing advanced technology and India's ambition in semiconductor manufacturing.
articles.trivia: Semiconductors are essential components in almost all modern electronic devices, from smartphones and computers to cars and critical infrastructure.

articles.headline: India Underscores Role in Global Food Security Amidst Rising Geopolitical Tensions
articles.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to contributing to global food security, especially for vulnerable nations, amidst ongoing geopolitical conflicts and climate challenges.
• The nation highlighted its significant agricultural production capacity and its willingness to share surplus food grains with countries in need.
• Discussions at an international forum focused on building resilient food supply chains and preventing disruptions caused by external shocks.
• India also emphasized the importance of sustainable agricultural practices and addressing trade barriers to ensure equitable access to food.
• This stance reinforces India's growing influence as a responsible global actor and a provider of public goods.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's commitment: Contributing to global food security.
Key challenges to global food security: Geopolitical conflicts, climate change.
India's approach: Sharing surplus food grains, sustainable practices.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's evolving role in global food security, considering its domestic agricultural challenges and its potential as a major food producer.
Discuss the interlinkages between geopolitical tensions, climate change, and global food supply chains, and suggest strategies for building greater resilience.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international cooperation mechanisms in addressing food crises and ensuring equitable access to food for all nations.
articles.imageDescription: A globe with crops growing on it, symbolizing global food production and distribution, with India highlighted as a contributor.
articles.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat, making it a crucial player in global food markets.

articles.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Enhanced Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies
articles.explainer:
• An expert committee appointed by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs submitted its report recommending greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The report highlighted the critical need for ULBs to have independent revenue streams and improved access to capital markets for urban development projects.
• Recommendations include reforming property tax collection, allowing ULBs to issue municipal bonds more easily, and rationalizing inter-governmental fiscal transfers.
• Enhanced financial powers are deemed essential for ULBs to effectively manage urban infrastructure, provide public services, and implement smart city initiatives.
• The panel emphasized that financial empowerment is key to strengthening local self-governance and addressing rapid urbanization challenges.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Recommendation: Greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
Suggested reforms: Property tax, municipal bonds, fiscal transfers.
Objective: Improve urban infrastructure and public services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India concerning financial resources and their impact on urban governance and service delivery.
Discuss the recommendations of the expert panel for enhancing the financial autonomy of ULBs and evaluate their potential effectiveness in promoting sustainable urban development.
Examine the broader implications of financial decentralization for strengthening democratic governance at the local level and addressing the complexities of rapid urbanization.
articles.imageDescription: A city skyline at dusk, with abstract financial graphs overlaid, symbolizing urban development and municipal finance.
articles.trivia: Municipal bonds, which allow local governments to borrow money from investors, are increasingly being explored by Indian cities to finance urban infrastructure projects.

articles.headline: India Bolsters Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure Sectors
articles.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced new measures to bolster cybersecurity for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The enhanced framework focuses on protecting vital systems in energy, finance, telecommunications, and transport from sophisticated cyber threats.
• Key initiatives include mandatory cybersecurity audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter compliance norms for operators.
• This move comes amidst a rising global trend of cyberattacks targeting government and private sector infrastructure.
• Capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and awareness campaigns for critical infrastructure personnel are also part of the comprehensive strategy.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC's role: Protecting critical information infrastructure.
Critical sectors: Energy, finance, telecom, transport.
Measures: Audits, threat intelligence, compliance norms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and analyze the challenges in developing a robust cybersecurity framework.
Evaluate the role of the NCIIPC and other government agencies in safeguarding national digital assets and ensuring cyber resilience.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and academia to strengthen India's cybersecurity posture against advanced persistent threats.
articles.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed over a network of interconnected infrastructure elements (power grid, financial data, communication towers), symbolizing cybersecurity protection.
articles.trivia: The NCIIPC was established in 2014 as a nodal agency to protect critical information infrastructure in India.

articles.headline: Tourism Sector Shows Robust Revival, Government Announces New Promotion Strategies
articles.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has demonstrated a strong post-pandemic revival, with domestic and international tourist arrivals showing significant growth.
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled new strategies focusing on sustainable tourism, niche segments like eco-tourism and adventure tourism, and digital promotion.
• Initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' are being expanded to highlight lesser-known destinations and promote local economies.
• Infrastructure development, including improved connectivity and hospitality services, remains a key priority to enhance tourist experience.
• The sector is projected to be a major contributor to job creation and foreign exchange earnings, reinforcing its role in economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: Tourism sector showing robust revival.
New strategies: Sustainable tourism, niche segments, digital promotion.
Government initiatives: 'Dekho Apna Desh', 'Swadesh Darshan'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's tourism sector as a driver of economic growth, employment generation, and cultural exchange.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the government to promote sustainable tourism and attract diverse tourist segments, both domestic and international.
Examine the challenges faced by the tourism industry, including infrastructure gaps and environmental concerns, and suggest measures for its long-term sustainable development.
articles.imageDescription: A vibrant collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments, natural landscapes, and cultural festivals.
articles.trivia: India's 'Incredible India' campaign is a globally recognized initiative to promote tourism, showcasing the country's diverse cultural heritage and natural beauty.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unveils New Conservation Plan for Ancient Harappan Site
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced a comprehensive conservation and preservation plan for a recently excavated ancient Harappan site in Gujarat.
• The plan involves advanced scientific techniques for site mapping, artifact preservation, and documentation, ensuring the long-term protection of the heritage.
• The site, believed to be a significant urban center of the Indus Valley Civilization, offers new insights into Harappan trade networks and urban planning.
• Community engagement and local capacity building are integral parts of the plan to foster heritage awareness and sustainable tourism.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to protecting its rich archaeological heritage and promoting cultural tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Location of the newly conserved site: Gujarat.
Civilization: Harappan (Indus Valley Civilization).
Agency: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in enhancing our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization and its contributions to ancient urbanism and trade.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites in India and evaluate the strategies adopted by the ASI.
Examine the role of cultural heritage in national identity building and its potential for promoting tourism and local economic development.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of an excavated archaeological site, showing the remnants of ancient Harappan structures and pottery fragments.
articles.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization, also known as the Harappan Civilization, is one of the world's three early civilizations, along with ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia.

articles.headline: HAL Boosts Indigenous Fighter Jet Production to Meet IAF Requirements
articles.explainer:
• Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) announced plans to significantly ramp up the production of indigenous fighter jets to fulfill the Indian Air Force's requirements.
• This move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in the defense sector, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign imports.
• The focus is on increasing the manufacturing capacity for Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas variants and developing next-generation platforms.
• Investments are being made in advanced manufacturing technologies, supply chain localization, and skill development for defense production.
• This surge in domestic production enhances India's strategic autonomy and strengthens its defense industrial base.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Manufacturer: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
Aircraft: Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas.
Policy: Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) in defense.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of indigenous defense production, particularly in fighter jets, for India's national security and strategic autonomy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in the defense manufacturing sector, including technological advancements and supply chain management.
Evaluate the impact of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative on strengthening India's defense industrial base and fostering innovation in aerospace technology.
articles.imageDescription: An LCA Tejas fighter jet taking off, with the HAL logo in the foreground, symbolizing indigenous defense capabilities.
articles.trivia: The LCA Tejas is a single-engine, delta wing, multirole light fighter designed by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) and produced by HAL for the Indian Air Force and Navy.

highlights: New Delhi building collapse kills eleven, sparks debate on urban safety and construction norms.
RBI maintains repo rate at 6.5% amidst easing inflation concerns, signalling cautious approach.
India and France announce deepened space cooperation, including new joint missions for climate and maritime surveillance.
Supreme Court orders comprehensive review of Electoral Bond Scheme implementation, emphasizing transparency.
India's Green Hydrogen Mission gains momentum with new investments, aiming for global leadership in clean energy.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the recent building collapse in New Delhi, consider the following statements:
1. The incident primarily highlights issues related to unauthorized construction and lax enforcement of building codes.
2. Disaster management protocols in urban areas are deemed sufficient to handle such events.
3. Urban planning policies are being reviewed to incorporate stricter safety standards.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the article explicitly mentions 'unauthorized construction, structural integrity of old buildings, and lax enforcement of building codes'. Statement 3 is correct as 'Experts are calling for an immediate review of urban planning policies'. Statement 2 is incorrect; the incident implies that disaster management protocols might be insufficient or need improvement, rather than being deemed sufficient.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of India's Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model often highlighted internationally?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article mentions Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker as components of the Indian DPI model championed at the G20. While NOFN (now BharatNet) is a crucial part of digital infrastructure, it is generally considered the foundational network layer rather than a 'public infrastructure' service like the others.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Green Hydrogen Mission:
1. Its primary objective is to make India a net importer of green hydrogen.
2. Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water using renewable electricity.
3. The mission aims to contribute to India's energy security and decarbonization goals.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. The mission aims to make India a 'global hub for green hydrogen production and export', meaning a net exporter, not importer. Statement 2 is correct as green hydrogen is produced via electrolysis using renewable energy. Statement 3 is correct as the mission contributes to 'energy security and decarbonization goals'.

prelimsMocks.question: The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) primarily governs water sharing between which two countries?
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article explicitly states that 'Delegations from India and Pakistan met for technical discussions regarding various aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT)'. The treaty was signed between these two nations.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to India's space program, what is the primary objective of the upcoming Chandrayaan-4 mission?
options: (a) To launch a human spaceflight mission to the Moon.
(b) To establish a permanent lunar base.
(c) To retrieve lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis.
(d) To deploy a large space telescope in lunar orbit.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) To retrieve lunar soil and rock samples for scientific analysis.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The article states that ISRO is 'accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, targeting a lunar sample return' and 'This mission aims to retrieve lunar soil and rock samples for detailed scientific analysis'.

mainsMocks.question: The tragic building collapse in New Delhi underscores critical gaps in urban governance and infrastructure safety. Critically analyze the challenges in ensuring safe and sustainable urban development in India, and suggest comprehensive reforms to address issues of unauthorized construction, building code enforcement, and disaster preparedness. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS1: Urbanization, their problems and their remedies; GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation; GS3: Disaster and Disaster Management.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce the context of the Delhi building collapse. Discuss challenges like rapid urbanization, informal settlements, corruption, lack of skilled labor, outdated building codes, and weak enforcement. Suggest reforms: stringent regulatory frameworks, digital mapping of properties, public-private partnerships for safe housing, capacity building for municipal staff, community participation in monitoring, and robust disaster response mechanisms. Conclude with the importance of a holistic approach.

mainsMocks.question: India's ambition to become a global hub for green hydrogen production and its deepened space cooperation with France signify a strategic push for technological leadership and sustainable development. Discuss the multifaceted benefits of such initiatives for India's economy, energy security, and geopolitical standing, while also highlighting the associated challenges. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; GS3: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment; Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology.
mainsMocks.approach: Start by linking Green Hydrogen Mission and space cooperation to India's strategic goals. Benefits: Economic (new industries, job creation, export potential), Energy Security (reduced fossil fuel dependence), Geopolitical (soft power, global leadership, climate action). Challenges: Green Hydrogen (high initial cost, infrastructure, technology scale-up); Space (technological complexity, funding, international competition). Conclude by emphasizing the synergy between these initiatives for a 'Viksit Bharat'.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's directive for a comprehensive review of the Electoral Bond Scheme implementation reflects ongoing concerns about transparency in political funding. In this context, analyze the implications of opaque political funding for democratic governance in India and propose comprehensive reforms to ensure greater accountability and fairness in electoral processes. (250 words)
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure; Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act; Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions.
mainsMocks.approach: Begin with the SC's directive and the core issue of transparency in political funding. Implications: undue corporate influence, quid pro quo, uneven playing field, erosion of public trust, black money. Reforms: State funding of elections, cap on corporate donations, mandatory disclosure of donors/recipients, strengthening Election Commission's powers, digital payment for donations, electoral trusts, and public awareness campaigns. Conclude by stressing the need for a robust regulatory framework for a healthy democracy.

quickBytes.text: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) uses the repo rate as a key tool to control inflation and manage liquidity in the economy.
The Chandrayaan missions are India's lunar exploration program, with Chandrayaan-1 discovering water molecules on the Moon.
Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) refers to shared digital systems that are foundational for public and private service delivery.
Mangrove forests play a crucial role in coastal protection by stabilizing shorelines and acting as natural barriers against erosion and storm surges.
The 'Neighbourhood First' policy guides India's foreign relations with its immediate neighbors, emphasizing cooperation and mutual benefit.
Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) aims to develop the backbone necessary to support the integrated digital health infrastructure of the country.
The Indus Water Treaty (IWT) is a water-distribution treaty between India and Pakistan, brokered by the World Bank in 1960.
The Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme provides income support to all eligible landholding farmer families in India.
The National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 aims to transform India's education system to meet the demands of the 21st century.
The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage in India.

GS1.headline: Eleven Dead in Delhi Building Collapse: Calls for Stricter Urban Planning and Construction Norms
GS1.explainer:
• A residential building in a densely populated area of New Delhi collapsed, killing eleven people and injuring several others.
• The incident has reignited concerns over unauthorized construction, structural integrity of old buildings, and lax enforcement of building codes in urban areas.
• Authorities have launched an investigation into the cause, with initial reports suggesting structural weakness and illegal modifications.
• Experts are calling for an immediate review of urban planning policies, disaster management protocols, and accountability mechanisms for developers and municipal bodies.
• The tragedy highlights the urgent need for comprehensive urban renewal programs and safety audits across major Indian cities.
gsSubTopics: Urbanization
Problems and remedies
Disaster and Disaster Management
Social empowerment
Role of civil society
prelimsPoints: Location of incident: New Delhi.
Number of casualties: Eleven dead, several injured.
Key issues highlighted: Unauthorized construction, old infrastructure, enforcement of building codes.
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the challenges in urban governance and infrastructure development in India, using the Delhi building collapse as a case study.
Discuss measures to improve construction safety, ensure structural integrity of buildings, and enhance disaster response mechanisms in Indian cities.
Examine the role of municipal corporations, urban development authorities, and regulatory bodies in preventing such tragedies and ensuring accountability.
GS1.imageDescription: Rescue workers and emergency services at the site of a collapsed residential building in New Delhi, with debris scattered around.
GS1.trivia: Delhi is one of the most densely populated cities globally, with a significant portion of its housing stock being old or informally constructed.

prelimsSyllabus.subject: Social Issues
prelimsSyllabus.topic: Problems of urbanization and their remedies

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Unveils New Conservation Plan for Ancient Harappan Site
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced a comprehensive conservation and preservation plan for a recently excavated ancient Harappan site in Gujarat.
• The plan involves advanced scientific techniques for site mapping, artifact preservation, and documentation, ensuring the long-term protection of the heritage.
• The site, believed to be a significant urban center of the Indus Valley Civilization, offers new insights into Harappan trade networks and urban planning.
• Community engagement and local capacity building are integral parts of the plan to foster heritage awareness and sustainable tourism.
• The initiative underscores India's commitment to protecting its rich archaeological heritage and promoting cultural tourism.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.
Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
prelimsPoints: Location of the newly conserved site: Gujarat.
Civilization: Harappan (Indus Valley Civilization).
Agency: Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of recent archaeological discoveries in enhancing our understanding of the Indus Valley Civilization and its contributions to ancient urbanism and trade.
Analyze the challenges involved in the conservation and preservation of ancient archaeological sites in India and evaluate the strategies adopted by the ASI.
Examine the role of cultural heritage in national identity building and its potential for promoting tourism and local economic development.
GS1.imageDescription: An aerial view of an excavated archaeological site, showing the remnants of ancient Harappan structures and pottery fragments.
GS1.trivia: The Indus Valley Civilization, also known as the Harappan Civilization, is one of the world's three early civilizations, along with ancient Egypt and Mesopotamia.

GS2.headline: India and France Deepen Strategic Cooperation in Space Sector with New Joint Missions
GS2.explainer:
• India and France announced enhanced collaboration in space technology, including new joint satellite missions for climate monitoring and maritime surveillance.
• The partnership aims to leverage ISRO's launch capabilities and CNES's expertise in satellite technology and data analysis.
• Key areas of cooperation include Earth observation, deep space exploration, and human spaceflight programs.
• This strategic alliance underscores both nations' commitment to sustainable space utilization and addressing global challenges like climate change.
• Training and exchange programs for scientists and engineers are also part of the expanded agreement, fostering knowledge transfer.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Partner countries: India and France.
Key space agencies involved: ISRO (India) and CNES (France).
Focus areas: Climate monitoring, maritime surveillance, Earth observation, deep space, human spaceflight.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-France cooperation in the space sector for India's strategic autonomy and its role in addressing global challenges.
Discuss how bilateral space partnerships contribute to technological advancement, economic growth, and diplomatic influence for both nations.
Evaluate the potential for India to emerge as a global leader in space technology through such international collaborations.
GS2.imageDescription: A composite image showing the flags of India and France with satellites orbiting Earth in the background, symbolizing space cooperation.
GS2.trivia: India and France have a long-standing history of cooperation in space, dating back to the 1960s, making France one of India's oldest partners in this domain.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Orders Comprehensive Review of Electoral Bond Scheme Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court of India directed the Election Commission and relevant government agencies to submit a comprehensive report on the implementation and impact of the now-scrapped Electoral Bond scheme.
• This directive follows concerns raised about transparency in political funding and the potential for quid pro quo arrangements.
• The court emphasized the need for full disclosure of all data related to electoral bonds, including donor and recipient details, which were previously withheld.
• The ruling aims to ensure accountability and uphold the principles of free and fair elections, crucial for democratic integrity.
• This move could pave the way for further legislative or judicial interventions to reform political funding mechanisms in India.
gsSubTopics: Indian Constitution—historical underpinnings, evolution, features, amendments, significant provisions and basic structure
Separation of powers between various organs dispute redressal mechanisms and institutions
Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
prelimsPoints: The Electoral Bond scheme was previously scrapped by the Supreme Court.
The court has now ordered a comprehensive review of its implementation.
Key bodies involved: Election Commission, government agencies.
mainsPoints: Examine the implications of the Supreme Court's directive on electoral bonds for transparency in political funding and democratic governance in India.
Discuss the challenges in regulating political funding in India and suggest reforms to enhance accountability and curb illegal practices.
Analyze the role of the judiciary in safeguarding democratic principles and ensuring fair electoral processes.
GS2.imageDescription: The Supreme Court of India building, symbolizing justice and judicial oversight in democratic processes.
GS2.trivia: The Electoral Bond Scheme, introduced in 2017, allowed individuals and corporate entities to donate to political parties anonymously, a provision that faced significant legal scrutiny.

GS2.headline: India Champions Digital Public Infrastructure Model at G20 Finance Ministers Meeting
GS2.explainer:
• India advocated for the global adoption of its Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) model at the G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors meeting.
• The Indian DPI model, encompassing Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, has been lauded for its success in driving financial inclusion and digital governance.
• India emphasized the potential of DPIs to accelerate sustainable development goals in developing nations by providing accessible and interoperable digital services.
• Discussions focused on funding mechanisms, technical assistance, and policy frameworks to help other countries implement similar digital ecosystems.
• The initiative positions India as a leader in digital transformation and a proponent of open-source, inclusive technological solutions.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's DPI examples: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker.
Forum where India championed DPI: G20 Finance Ministers meeting.
DPI aims to accelerate Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).
mainsPoints: Elaborate on the concept of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) and analyze how India's model has contributed to financial inclusion and digital governance.
Discuss the potential and challenges of replicating India's DPI model in other developing countries, considering socio-economic and regulatory differences.
Examine India's growing role as a leader in digital innovation and its implications for global technological cooperation and soft power.
GS2.imageDescription: A graphic depicting interconnected digital platforms like Aadhaar, UPI, and DigiLocker, with the G20 logo in the background.
GS2.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processed over 130 billion transactions in 2024, making it one of the most successful digital payment systems globally.

GS2.headline: India Pledges Continued Humanitarian Assistance to Afghanistan Amidst Crisis
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to providing humanitarian aid to Afghanistan, focusing on food security, medical supplies, and educational support.
• The pledge comes amidst ongoing humanitarian crisis in Afghanistan, exacerbated by economic challenges and political instability.
• India emphasized its 'people-first' approach, ensuring that aid reaches the most vulnerable sections of the Afghan population directly.
• Discussions with international partners are underway to ensure effective delivery mechanisms and coordination of relief efforts.
• While maintaining a cautious stance on political recognition, India continues to engage on humanitarian grounds, reflecting its neighborhood first policy.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Recipient country: Afghanistan.
India's aid focus: Food, medical supplies, education.
India's foreign policy principle: 'Neighbourhood First'.
mainsPoints: Analyze the complexities of India's foreign policy towards Afghanistan in the current geopolitical scenario, balancing humanitarian concerns with strategic interests.
Discuss the challenges in delivering humanitarian aid to conflict-affected regions and the importance of international cooperation in such efforts.
Evaluate the 'Neighbourhood First' policy and its application in addressing crises in India's immediate vicinity.
GS2.imageDescription: A symbolic image of hands extending aid to a silhouette of Afghanistan, representing India's humanitarian efforts.
GS2.trivia: India has historically been a significant development partner for Afghanistan, undertaking various infrastructure projects and providing capacity building support.

GS2.headline: Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission Expands Services, Focuses on Interoperability
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced an expansion of its services, aiming to create a seamless online platform for healthcare.
• Key focus areas include enhancing the interoperability of digital health records and integrating more private healthcare providers into the ecosystem.
• The mission seeks to empower citizens with greater control over their health data and facilitate easier access to healthcare services across the country.
• New features are being rolled out, such as advanced health locker functionalities and telemedicine integration with primary health centers.
• This expansion is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving healthcare delivery efficiency in India.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: ABDM aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Key aspects: Interoperability of health records, integration of private providers.
Empowers citizens with control over health data.
mainsPoints: Evaluate the potential of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission to transform India's healthcare landscape and address existing disparities in access and quality.
Discuss the challenges associated with implementing a national digital health mission, including data privacy, cybersecurity, and digital literacy.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access to digital health services and promote the adoption of ABDM across diverse socio-economic groups.
GS2.imageDescription: A visual representation of interconnected digital health services, with a mobile phone displaying a health app and a doctor consulting via telemedicine.
GS2.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission was launched nationwide in September 2021, building on the pilot project initiated in six Union Territories.

GS2.headline: India and Pakistan Hold Technical Talks on Indus Water Treaty Issues
GS2.explainer:
• Delegations from India and Pakistan met for technical discussions regarding various aspects of the Indus Water Treaty (IWT).
• The talks focused on resolving disputes related to the design of hydroelectric projects on the western rivers, as per the treaty provisions.
• Both sides reiterated their commitment to upholding the IWT, a landmark agreement governing transboundary river water sharing.
• While no major breakthroughs were reported, the engagement is seen as crucial for maintaining dialogue and managing water resources cooperatively.
• The Permanent Indus Commission, established under the treaty, facilitates these annual meetings and technical exchanges.
gsSubTopics: India and its neighborhood- relations
Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
prelimsPoints: Indus Water Treaty (IWT) signed between India and Pakistan.
Western Rivers: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab (Pakistan's exclusive use).
Eastern Rivers: Ravi, Beas, Sutlej (India's exclusive use).
Forum for talks: Permanent Indus Commission.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the Indus Water Treaty in India-Pakistan relations and the challenges in its implementation amidst evolving geopolitical dynamics.
Analyze the role of transboundary water agreements in fostering regional cooperation and preventing conflicts over shared natural resources.
Evaluate the implications of climate change and increasing water scarcity on the long-term viability and effectiveness of the IWT.
GS2.imageDescription: A map illustrating the Indus River System and the division of rivers as per the Indus Water Treaty between India and Pakistan.
GS2.trivia: The Indus Water Treaty, brokered by the World Bank, was signed in 1960 and is considered one of the most successful water-sharing treaties in the world, having survived multiple conflicts between India and Pakistan.

GS2.headline: National Education Policy 2020: Review of Implementation Progress and Challenges
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education conducted a comprehensive review of the implementation progress of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020.
• The review highlighted advancements in curriculum reforms, vocational education integration, and digital learning initiatives.
• Key challenges identified include infrastructure gaps, teacher training requirements, and ensuring equitable access to quality education across all regions.
• Stakeholders, including state governments, educational institutions, and civil society organizations, participated in the consultative meeting.
• The government reaffirmed its commitment to the NEP's transformative vision, emphasizing flexible learning pathways and multidisciplinary education.
gsSubTopics: Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
prelimsPoints: NEP 2020 aims for comprehensive reform in education.
Progress areas: Curriculum, vocational education, digital learning.
Challenges: Infrastructure, teacher training, equitable access.
mainsPoints: Analyze the transformative potential of the National Education Policy 2020 in reshaping India's education system and fostering a knowledge-based society.
Discuss the major challenges in the effective implementation of NEP 2020, particularly concerning resource allocation, teacher capacity building, and digital divide.
Suggest strategies for state governments and educational institutions to overcome these challenges and ensure the successful realization of NEP's objectives.
GS2.imageDescription: A group of diverse students in a modern classroom setting, with digital learning tools, symbolizing the holistic and inclusive vision of NEP 2020.
GS2.trivia: The National Education Policy 2020 is the third major education policy in India's history, succeeding the policies of 1968 and 1986 (modified in 1992).

GS2.headline: India Underscores Role in Global Food Security Amidst Rising Geopolitical Tensions
GS2.explainer:
• India reiterated its commitment to contributing to global food security, especially for vulnerable nations, amidst ongoing geopolitical conflicts and climate challenges.
• The nation highlighted its significant agricultural production capacity and its willingness to share surplus food grains with countries in need.
• Discussions at an international forum focused on building resilient food supply chains and preventing disruptions caused by external shocks.
• India also emphasized the importance of sustainable agricultural practices and addressing trade barriers to ensure equitable access to food.
• This stance reinforces India's growing influence as a responsible global actor and a provider of public goods.
gsSubTopics: Important International institutions, agencies and fora, their structure, mandate
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India's commitment: Contributing to global food security.
Key challenges to global food security: Geopolitical conflicts, climate change.
India's approach: Sharing surplus food grains, sustainable practices.
mainsPoints: Analyze India's evolving role in global food security, considering its domestic agricultural challenges and its potential as a major food producer.
Discuss the interlinkages between geopolitical tensions, climate change, and global food supply chains, and suggest strategies for building greater resilience.
Evaluate the effectiveness of international cooperation mechanisms in addressing food crises and ensuring equitable access to food for all nations.
GS2.imageDescription: A globe with crops growing on it, symbolizing global food production and distribution, with India highlighted as a contributor.
GS2.trivia: India is the world's second-largest producer of rice and wheat, making it a crucial player in global food markets.

GS2.headline: Expert Panel Recommends Enhanced Financial Autonomy for Urban Local Bodies
GS2.explainer:
• An expert committee appointed by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs submitted its report recommending greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
• The report highlighted the critical need for ULBs to have independent revenue streams and improved access to capital markets for urban development projects.
• Recommendations include reforming property tax collection, allowing ULBs to issue municipal bonds more easily, and rationalizing inter-governmental fiscal transfers.
• Enhanced financial powers are deemed essential for ULBs to effectively manage urban infrastructure, provide public services, and implement smart city initiatives.
• The panel emphasized that financial empowerment is key to strengthening local self-governance and addressing rapid urbanization challenges.
gsSubTopics: Functions and responsibilities of the Union and the States, issues and challenges pertaining to the federal structure, devolution of powers and finances up to local levels and challenges therein
Urbanization, their problems and their remedies
prelimsPoints: Recommendation: Greater financial autonomy for Urban Local Bodies (ULBs).
Suggested reforms: Property tax, municipal bonds, fiscal transfers.
Objective: Improve urban infrastructure and public services.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges faced by Urban Local Bodies (ULBs) in India concerning financial resources and their impact on urban governance and service delivery.
Discuss the recommendations of the expert panel for enhancing the financial autonomy of ULBs and evaluate their potential effectiveness in promoting sustainable urban development.
Examine the broader implications of financial decentralization for strengthening democratic governance at the local level and addressing the complexities of rapid urbanization.
GS2.imageDescription: A city skyline at dusk, with abstract financial graphs overlaid, symbolizing urban development and municipal finance.
GS2.trivia: Municipal bonds, which allow local governments to borrow money from investors, are increasingly being explored by Indian cities to finance urban infrastructure projects.

GS3.headline: RBI Maintains Status Quo on Repo Rate Amidst Easing Inflation Concerns
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) concluded its Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting, deciding to keep the repo rate unchanged at 6.5%.
• This decision comes as inflation shows signs of moderation, though global uncertainties continue to pose risks to economic stability.
• The RBI reiterated its commitment to supporting growth while ensuring inflation remains within the target band of 2-6%.
• Analysts suggest the central bank is adopting a cautious wait-and-watch approach, assessing the impact of previous rate hikes and global economic trends.
• Liquidity conditions in the banking system were also discussed, with the RBI affirming its stance on managing surplus liquidity effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy
Monetary policy
Inflation
Growth and development
prelimsPoints: Current Repo Rate: 6.5% (unchanged).
Inflation target band for RBI: 2-6%.
MPC meetings are held at least four times a year.
mainsPoints: Analyze the factors influencing the RBI's decision to maintain the status quo on the repo rate and its implications for economic growth and inflation in India.
Discuss the challenges faced by the RBI in balancing inflation control with the imperative of fostering economic growth in the current global economic climate.
Evaluate the effectiveness of monetary policy tools in addressing supply-side inflation and external shocks.
GS3.imageDescription: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) logo, symbolizing financial stability and economic governance.
GS3.trivia: The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) in India consists of six members: three officials from the RBI and three external members nominated by the Government of India.

GS3.headline: India's Green Hydrogen Mission Gains Momentum with New Investment Pledges
GS3.explainer:
• The National Green Hydrogen Mission is witnessing significant progress, attracting substantial investment pledges from both domestic and international players.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, contributing to energy security and decarbonization goals.
• New policy incentives and a streamlined regulatory framework are being developed to facilitate faster project execution and technology adoption.
• Several pilot projects for green hydrogen production and its applications in sectors like refining, fertilizers, and steel are underway.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its ambitious climate targets and transition towards a sustainable energy future.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
prelimsPoints: Objective of the mission: Make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy sources.
Applications include refining, fertilizers, steel, and transportation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of the National Green Hydrogen Mission for India's energy security, climate goals, and economic growth.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities in scaling up green hydrogen production and its integration into various industrial sectors in India.
Evaluate the role of government policies, private investments, and technological innovation in achieving the mission's objectives.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration depicting green hydrogen production facilities with wind turbines and solar panels in the background, symbolizing renewable energy.
GS3.trivia: Green hydrogen is produced by splitting water into hydrogen and oxygen using renewable electricity, a process called electrolysis.

GS3.headline: ISRO Gears Up for Chandrayaan-4 Mission, Focusing on Lunar Sample Return
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced accelerated preparations for its ambitious Chandrayaan-4 mission, targeting a lunar sample return.
• This mission aims to retrieve lunar soil and rock samples for detailed scientific analysis, providing insights into the Moon's origin and evolution.
• Chandrayaan-4 is envisioned as a complex multi-component mission, potentially involving a lander, rover, and an ascent module to bring samples back to Earth.
• The mission builds upon the successes of Chandrayaan-1 and Chandrayaan-2, advancing India's capabilities in planetary exploration.
• International collaborations are also being explored to enhance the mission's scientific scope and technological feasibility.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Chandrayaan-4's primary objective: Lunar sample return.
Previous Chandrayaan missions: Chandrayaan-1 (2008), Chandrayaan-2 (2019).
Key components: Lander, Rover, Ascent Module (expected).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of India's lunar exploration program, particularly missions like Chandrayaan-4, for scientific advancement and geopolitical standing.
Analyze the technological challenges involved in a lunar sample return mission and how ISRO is addressing them.
Evaluate the broader implications of India's growing capabilities in space technology for its economic development and international collaborations.
GS3.imageDescription: An artistic rendition of the Chandrayaan-4 lander and rover on the lunar surface, collecting samples, with Earth visible in the distance.
GS3.trivia: The first successful lunar sample return mission was by the Soviet Union's Luna 16 in 1970.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Scheme to Combat Coastal Erosion on Eastern Coast
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change launched a new comprehensive scheme to mitigate coastal erosion along India's eastern coastline.
• The scheme focuses on a multi-pronged approach, including natural solutions like mangrove restoration and artificial interventions such as seawalls and groynes.
• Climate change-induced sea-level rise and increased frequency of cyclones are exacerbating coastal erosion, threatening livelihoods and ecosystems.
• The initiative will also involve community participation and capacity building for local populations to adapt to changing coastal dynamics.
• Funding will be allocated for research and development into innovative erosion control technologies and monitoring systems.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Disaster and Disaster Management
prelimsPoints: Focus of the scheme: Coastal erosion mitigation on India's eastern coastline.
Approaches: Mangrove restoration (natural), seawalls, groynes (artificial).
