Causes of erosion: Sea-level rise, cyclones.
mainsPoints: Analyze the causes and consequences of coastal erosion in India, particularly along the eastern coast, and discuss the effectiveness of various mitigation strategies.
Evaluate the importance of integrating traditional knowledge with scientific approaches in coastal zone management and disaster preparedness.
Discuss the challenges in implementing large-scale environmental protection schemes and ensuring community participation for long-term sustainability.
GS3.imageDescription: A coastline showing a combination of restored mangroves and engineered structures like seawalls protecting against erosion, with the sea in the background.
GS3.trivia: Approximately one-third of India's coastline is experiencing varying degrees of erosion, with the eastern coast being particularly vulnerable due to its geological features and exposure to Bay of Bengal cyclones.

GS3.headline: PM-KISAN Scheme Undergoes Enhanced Beneficiary Verification to Curb Irregularities
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government initiated an enhanced verification drive for beneficiaries under the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme.
• The move aims to eliminate ineligible beneficiaries and curb irregularities, ensuring that financial aid reaches genuine farmers.
• Leveraging Aadhaar-based authentication and land record digitization, the verification process seeks to improve transparency and efficiency.
• Farmers found ineligible are being asked to return the disbursed funds, with provisions for recovery mechanisms.
• This measure is part of broader efforts to streamline direct benefit transfer (DBT) schemes and optimize public expenditure.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers

Direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System—objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
prelimsPoints: PM-KISAN scheme provides income support to farmer families.
Verification methods: Aadhaar-based authentication, land record digitization.
Aim: Eliminate ineligible beneficiaries and curb irregularities.
mainsPoints: Critically evaluate the effectiveness of direct benefit transfer (DBT) schemes like PM-KISAN in achieving their objectives and addressing agrarian distress.
Discuss the challenges in identifying genuine beneficiaries and preventing leakages in welfare schemes, and suggest technological and administrative solutions.
Analyze the broader implications of enhanced verification drives for public expenditure management and the promotion of good governance in India.
GS3.imageDescription: A farmer working in a field, with a digital overlay symbolizing direct benefit transfer and Aadhaar verification, highlighting the modernization of agricultural support.
GS3.trivia: PM-KISAN scheme provides an income support of ₹6,000 per year in three equal installments of ₹2,000 every four months to eligible farmer families.

GS3.headline: India Aims to Strengthen Domestic Chip Design Ecosystem with New Incentives
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology announced new incentives to bolster India's domestic semiconductor chip design ecosystem.
• The initiative aims to attract global players and nurture local talent, reducing India's reliance on imported chips.
• Focus areas include providing financial support for R&D, establishing state-of-the-art design centers, and fostering academia-industry collaboration.
• This move is critical for India's strategic autonomy in electronics manufacturing and its ambition to become a global semiconductor hub.
• Skill development programs and specialized courses in VLSI design and embedded systems are also being promoted.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
prelimsPoints: Objective: Strengthen domestic semiconductor chip design ecosystem.
Measures: Financial support, design centers, academia-industry collaboration.
Goal: Reduce reliance on imported chips, achieve strategic autonomy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of developing a robust domestic semiconductor chip design and manufacturing ecosystem for India's economic growth and national security.
Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in attracting global semiconductor investments and nurturing local talent in this highly capital-intensive sector.
Evaluate the effectiveness of government incentive schemes and policy frameworks in achieving India's ambition to become a global semiconductor hub.
GS3.imageDescription: A stylized microchip with circuits, representing advanced technology and India's ambition in semiconductor manufacturing.
GS3.trivia: Semiconductors are essential components in almost all modern electronic devices, from smartphones and computers to cars and critical infrastructure.

GS3.headline: India Bolsters Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure Sectors
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced new measures to bolster cybersecurity for critical infrastructure sectors.
• The enhanced framework focuses on protecting vital systems in energy, finance, telecommunications, and transport from sophisticated cyber threats.
• Key initiatives include mandatory cybersecurity audits, real-time threat intelligence sharing, and stricter compliance norms for operators.
• This move comes amidst a rising global trend of cyberattacks targeting government and private sector infrastructure.
• Capacity building for cybersecurity professionals and awareness campaigns for critical infrastructure personnel are also part of the comprehensive strategy.
gsSubTopics: Challenges to internal security through communication networks, role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges, basics of cyber security; money-laundering and its prevention
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
keyTerms: National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC)
prelimsPoints: NCIIPC's role: Protecting critical information infrastructure.
Critical sectors: Energy, finance, telecom, transport.
Measures: Audits, threat intelligence, compliance norms.
mainsPoints: Discuss the evolving nature of cyber threats to India's critical infrastructure and analyze the challenges in developing a robust cybersecurity framework.
Evaluate the role of the NCIIPC and other government agencies in safeguarding national digital assets and ensuring cyber resilience.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and academia to strengthen India's cybersecurity posture against advanced persistent threats.
GS3.imageDescription: A digital padlock icon superimposed over a network of interconnected infrastructure elements (power grid, financial data, communication towers), symbolizing cybersecurity protection.
GS3.trivia: The NCIIPC was established in 2014 as a nodal agency to protect critical information infrastructure in India.

GS3.headline: Tourism Sector Shows Robust Revival, Government Announces New Promotion Strategies
GS3.explainer:
• India's tourism sector has demonstrated a strong post-pandemic revival, with domestic and international tourist arrivals showing significant growth.
• The Ministry of Tourism unveiled new strategies focusing on sustainable tourism, niche segments like eco-tourism and adventure tourism, and digital promotion.
• Initiatives like 'Dekho Apna Desh' and 'Swadesh Darshan' are being expanded to highlight lesser-known destinations and promote local economies.
• Infrastructure development, including improved connectivity and hospitality services, remains a key priority to enhance tourist experience.
• The sector is projected to be a major contributor to job creation and foreign exchange earnings, reinforcing its role in economic growth.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Effects of liberalization on the economy, changes in industrial policy and their effects on industrial growth
prelimsPoints: Tourism sector showing robust revival.
New strategies: Sustainable tourism, niche segments, digital promotion.
Government initiatives: 'Dekho Apna Desh', 'Swadesh Darshan'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of India's tourism sector as a driver of economic growth, employment generation, and cultural exchange.
Analyze the strategies adopted by the government to promote sustainable tourism and attract diverse tourist segments, both domestic and international.
Examine the challenges faced by the tourism industry, including infrastructure gaps and environmental concerns, and suggest measures for its long-term sustainable development.
GS3.imageDescription: A vibrant collage of famous Indian tourist destinations, including historical monuments, natural landscapes, and cultural festivals.
GS3.trivia: India's 'Incredible India' campaign is a globally recognized initiative to promote tourism, showcasing the country's diverse cultural heritage and natural beauty.

GS3.headline: HAL Boosts Indigenous Fighter Jet Production to Meet IAF Requirements
GS3.explainer:
• Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) announced plans to significantly ramp up the production of indigenous fighter jets to fulfill the Indian Air Force's requirements.
• This move aligns with the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' (Self-Reliant India) initiative in the defense sector, aiming to reduce reliance on foreign imports.
• The focus is on increasing the manufacturing capacity for Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas variants and developing next-generation platforms.
• Investments are being made in advanced manufacturing technologies, supply chain localization, and skill development for defense production.
• This surge in domestic production enhances India's strategic autonomy and strengthens its defense industrial base.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Security challenges and their management in border areas – linkages of organized crime with terrorism
prelimsPoints: Manufacturer: Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL).
Aircraft: Light Combat Aircraft (LCA) Tejas.
Policy: Atmanirbhar Bharat (Self-Reliant India) in defense.
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of indigenous defense production, particularly in fighter jets, for India's national security and strategic autonomy.
Discuss the challenges and opportunities in achieving self-reliance in the defense manufacturing sector, including technological advancements and supply chain management.
Evaluate the impact of the 'Atmanirbhar Bharat' initiative on strengthening India's defense industrial base and fostering innovation in aerospace technology.
GS3.imageDescription: An LCA Tejas fighter jet taking off, with the HAL logo in the foreground, symbolizing indigenous defense capabilities.
GS3.trivia: The LCA Tejas is a single-engine, delta wing, multirole light fighter designed by the Aeronautical Development Agency (ADA) and produced by HAL for the Indian Air Force and Navy.
